versions.label: LSB
versions.text: "By this all men will know that you are My disciples, if you have love for one another."

context_lines: In **John 13**, Jesus prepares His disciples for His departure by washing their feet, exposing betrayal, and commanding them to love one another. The chapter frames Christian identity around the pattern and love of Christ Himself.
explanation_lines: **ἀγάπη (*agapē* (ah-GAH-pay))** — love — is the identifying mark of a disciple. Not title, not performance, but self-giving love in action.
Salvation rests on **ὁμολογέω (*homologeō* (hoh-mol-oh-GEH-oh))** — confession — and **πιστεύω (*pisteuō* (pis-TYOO-oh))** — belief. These are the foundation from which discipleship flows.
